observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant continue reconciling the variance
between the GL and SL of the CIP account by identifying the
projects in the former and individually trace it back from the
latter. Note the projects in the GL that have no corresponding
entries in the SL as reconciling items and make an equivalent
record; and
b. Municipal Engineer retrieve documents pertaining to the long-
outstanding CIP accounts as provided in Annex A such as, but
not limited to the SWA, Certificate of Completion, Results of
Project Inspection, SB issuances as to completion or
abandonment of projects, etc. Make an actual/ocular
inspection and assessment of these projects vis-à-vis the
documents at hand. Submit to the Accounting the results of
such inspection/assessment which will be the basis of the
Accountant to make adjustments in the books of accounts.

2.6 We recommended that Management direct:
1. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to effect the fund
transfer the amount of P1,000,000.00 as municipality’s
counterpart to fund the project which incurred overdraft and
the former to ensure that funds are available before making
payment to avoid incurring negative balance of an account;
2. The Municipal Accountant to continue in tracing back the
Prior Year’s subsidiary accounts to establish the details of the
recorded balances under the Due to NGAs account;
3. The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal
Engineer, to make a follow up with the contractor if the
deficiency noted by the Source Agency was already complied
and eventually require the same to submit the lacking
document for the final billing payment, else, the fund balance
will be returned to the Source Agency if utilization of this fund
is no longer possible;
4. The Municipal Accountant to clarify with the RHU Tubajon
and/or PHIC personnel whether the program undertakings
with the source agency covering the depository account
maintained by the LGU is still active and continue to be
implemented, else, the balances will be returned to the source
agency and close the depository accounts. Ensure that the said
program undertakings will be supported with MOA; and
5. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to return the
unexpended balances of fund transfers with subject amount of
P58.73 and P1,287.30 to DILG and DBM respectively which
programs/projects have already been completed at year end;
and
6. The Municipal Accountant to conduct regular close monitoring
of the status on the individual subsidiary ledger accounts
composing the Due to NGAs account to avoid
misrepresentation of the accounts in the FS.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Trace and evaluate the transactions with negative balances
reflected in the Schedule/Subsidiary Ledgers of the Accounts
Payable with subject amount of P5,420,364.43 and effect
necessary adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies; and
b. Revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund the
unclaimed outstanding Accounts Payable aged two (2) years or
more and with subject amount of P4,919,269.26 to reflect the
correct balance of the account.

4.8 We recommended that the Accountant:
a. Continue reconciling the balances of the subsidiary and general
ledgers by tracing all the transactions in the latter from the
former. Note the entries in the general ledger that have no
corresponding records in the subsidiary ledger as reconciling
items;
b. Substantiate the account balances without schedule and
supporting documents, including those financial assistance with
incomplete information like absence of payee. The result of the
action may be the basis of the request for write-off to be filed
by the LGU before COA; and
c. Send demand letters to the recipients requiring immediate
liquidation of the financial assistance received. Inform them of
possible consequences if the requests remained unheeded like
barring to receive another grants from the LGU or the filing of
appropriate legal actions against them.

5.6 We recommended that the:
a. MENRO submit the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA)
covering the implementation of the project to the Municipal
Accountant as the latter’s basis to derecognize the inventory
account that transformed into reforestation project and
recognized biological assets - Plants and Trees account; and
b. On the basis of the SWA, the Municipal Accountant to draw
Journal Voucher (derecognize the issued and planted seedlings)
to adjust the affected inventory, and Plant and Trees account
under the Biological Assets amounting to P3,008,390.00; and
c. MENRO, as focal person of the program, properly monitor the
project and provide maintenance to protect against possible
loss or destruction. In the event that mortality is noted on the
trees planted, prepare a report so that proper adjustment or
derecognition will be made

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the land
embankment from Investment Property, Land account to
Other Land Improvements

8.7 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Accountant to record the AICS payments to Donations instead of
OMOE account and the Municipal Budget Officer to obligate the
same in the Donations registries of the office.

9.9 We recommended that the:
a. MENRO to submit to the Auditor the latest SWA of the
project;
b. Management to refrain from making advance payments to any
implementation of LGU programs, projects, and activities,
other than the allowed mobilization amount and in situations
prescribed in the prevailing laws, rules and regulations; and
c. Management to consistently monitor the progress of the project
to ensure timely and full implementation thereof.

10.6 We recommended that the BAC Chairperson/GSO ensures the
correctness and consistency of information provided in the BAC
Resolutions, the ones posted in the PhilGEPS, and the ones
indicated in the PO.

12.8 We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that the number of vacation leave and sick leave after
monetization shall be at least five (5);
b. Make sure that all payments for monetization are supported
with complete and valid documents as mentioned in Item 5.4 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
c. Advice the Human Resource Management Officer to ascertain
that applied days for monetization are charged first against
vacation leave, prior to the sick leave if the latter is insufficient,
a mandatory balance of least five (5) leave credits after
monetization must be observed.

13.11 We recommended that Management direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Monthly
Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA on or
before 15th day after the end of each month and to
immediately prepare and maintain separate Subsidiary
Ledgers of prior years’ MDRRMF unexpended balances by
year of transfer for easy determination and monitoring on the
status of funds; and
b. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to ensure transfer of
the unexpended balance of the MDRRMF to Special Trust
Fund at year-end to reflect the correct balances of the Cash,
Liability and Equity Accounts in the Financial Statements and
to make sure of cash back-up for DRRM programs and
projects.

14.5 We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that cash assistance to barangays be covered with MOA
to provide guidelines on how the funds be utilized and
reported; and
b. Advise the Accountant to record the cash assistance as
receivable, instead of an expense account, for proper monitoring
thereof and uphold accountability of the funds.

15.6 We recommended the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to submit to COA all his cash advances
vouchers and the corresponding liquidations and supporting
documents without delay for the post audit of transactions,
otherwise the accounts will be re-evaluated on the basis of
available information; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit all the
transaction and financial reports to the Auditor in a manner
prescribed in Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 70 of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

The Municipal Accountant immediately prepare a JEV for correcting/adjusting entries of the reconciling items to adjust the balance of the cash in bank of accounts as per book and to bring the account into agreement with the depository bank and for fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

The Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPE and inventory as of December 31, 2020 and years thereafter. Prepare the RPCPPE & RPCI, reconcile the reports with the accounting office and effect the appropriate adjustments, if there are any, for a reliable and fair presentation of the accounts in the Statement of Financial Position. Also, submit the report to the Office of the Audit Team Leader for evaluation and reference in audit.
observations.reason: Physical count of PPE and inventories is still ongoing. Moreover, RPCPPE and RPCI were not submitted in the Office of the Auditor for review and verification.

observations.recommendation: a. The Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local roads of the jurisdiction of the municipality and render a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Networks System; b. The Municipal General Service Officer – Designate to: Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map; Regularly maintains and updates the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads; Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; c. The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Service Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and d. The Municipal Accountant to: Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card; Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; Disclose in the Notes to FS the length covered by the system, additions and reductions, major repairs and regular maintenance for the year.
observations.reason: Inventory of the local road courses GSO- Designate, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant Implemented is still on-going as of date, hence other courses of actions were not undertaken.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and regularly compute, on a monthly basis, the depreciation of all depreciable assets, in order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Various PPE items were not yet depreciated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: The Management to call the attention of the concerned Accountable Officers for immediate liquidation for their long overdue cash advances or refund the unexpended amount in favor of the government.

The Management to: a. Strictly adhere to the provision of Section 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure the optimal utilization of 20 percent DF to help achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental management outcomes of the municipality; b. Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost as a result of delayed implementation; c. Inform the intended beneficiaries of the programmed projects so that proper coordination will facilitate in the execution of the projects and the benefits thereof at the earliest possible time; and d. Prepare a catch up plan and fast tract the implementation of the above-mentioned priority development projects.

The LSB incorporate in its succeeding budget programs and activities intended for ECCD to support the holistic needs of the young children of the municipality as mandated under section 7 (b) of RA No. 10410.

The Management to maximize the utilization of the LCPC funds through the implementation of LCPC related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objectives for which funds were provided.

The Management to ensure the financial assistance to barangays for the construction of the infrastructure projects be covered with Memorandum of Agreement to provide basis for monitoring the proper use of such government funds and be liquidated by the implementing agencies through the submission of required documents mentioned in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

1.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately
prepare a JEV for correcting/adjusting entries of the book
reconciling items to adjust the balance of the cash in bank per
book and to bring the account into agreement with the depository
bank and for fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial
Statements.

2.7 We recommended that the Inventory Committee complete the
physical count of PPE and inventory as of December 31, 2020 and
years thereafter. Prepare the RPCPPE & RPCI, reconcile the reports
with the accounting office and effect promptly appropriate
adjustments, if there are any, for a reliable and fair presentation of
the accounts in the Statement of Financial Position. Also, submit the
report to the Office of the Audit Team Leader for evaluation and
reference in audit.

3.5 We recommended that Management take the following courses of
action:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local
roads under the jurisdiction of the municipality and render a
Report on the Physical Count of the Road Networks System;
b. The Municipal General Service Officer – Designate to: Render a
Report on Local Road Network and Road Map; Regularly
maintains and updates the Registry of Public Infrastructures for
Roads; Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for
all roads and its components;
c. The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal General Service Officer with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects; and
d. The Municipal Accountant to: Prepare and maintain a Local Road
Network Ledger Card; Keep and maintain subsidiary records for
roads and road components for every road network; Disclose in
the Notes to FS the length covered by the system, additions and
reductions, major repairs and regular maintenance for the year.

4.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to effect the
necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and regularly
compute, on a monthly basis, the depreciation of all depreciable
assets, in order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial
statements.

5.7 We recommended that management call the attention of the
concerned Accountable Officers for immediate liquidation for
their long overdue cash Advances or refund the unexpended
amount in favour of the government.

6.7 We recommended that management:
a) Strictly adhere to the provision of Section 5 of DILG-DBM
JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure the
optimal utilization of 20 percent DF to help achieve the
desirable socio-economic and environmental management
outcomes of the municipality;
b) Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and
implementation of development projects to avoid incurring
possible additional cost as a result of delayed
implementation;
c) Inform the intended beneficiaries of the programmed
projects so that proper coordination will facilitate in the
execution of the projects and the benefits thereof at the
earliest possible time; and
d) Prepare a catch up plan and fast tract the implementation of
the above-mentioned priority development projects.

7.7 We recommended that the LSB incorporate in its succeeding budget
programs and activities intended for ECCD to support the holistic
needs of the young children of the municipality as mandated under
section 7 (b) of RA No. 10410.

8.7 We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of
the LCPC funds through the implementation of LCPC related
programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objectives
for which funds were provided.

9.8 We recommended that the financial assistance to barangays for
the construction of the infrastructure projects be covered with
Memorandum of Agreement to provide basis for monitoring the
proper use of such government funds and be liquidated by the
implementing agencies through the submission of required
documents mentioned in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June
14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement and effect immediately the adjustments in the accounts affected in order to present fairly the Cash-in-Bank- Local Currency Checking Account balance in the CY 2019 book Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepare the above mentioned certified list of taxpayers to be furnished the Municipal Accounting Office as basis for setting up the beginning balance of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax every year at an equal amount of one percent for each of the account out of the total amount due and collectible from taxpayers for a particular year. We further recommended that subsidiary ledgers/taxpayers records be updated and maintained for Local Government Units, Volume I.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of PPE, Inventory and Biological Assets at the end of every year in order that appropriate adjustments of the accounts affected be promptly effected, leading to a reliable and fair presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.reason: RPCPPE, RPCI and RPCBA were not submitted in the Office of the Auditor for review and verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate and render account for their long overdue cash Advances or refund in favor of the government the unliquidated amount, if applicable.
observations.reason: Demand Letters were sent to accountable officers, however not all were able to settle and liquidate their cash advances as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast track and prioritize the implementation of the above-mentioned projects in order that the intended beneficiaries can avail of the benefits of the projects.

We recommended that Management identify qualified stakeholders to be appointed as members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in order for the same to properly discharge the mandated function of the organization.

We recommended that Management give also priority to the ECCD programs, projects and activities in the formulation of SEF Budget in order not to deprive the schoolchildren of the intended benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary documents and henceforth, to meticulously check the supporting documents to ensure the propriety, validity and completeness of claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant withheld the creditable VAT and other percentage taxes on their purchases of goods or services in accordance with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 and Section 80 of the Tax Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Management agreed to require the members of the BAC to adhere to COA Circular No. 76-41 dated July 30, 1976 and Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the general policy on prohibition of splitting of contracts and competitive bidding.

We recommended that Management intensify its control of collection of water bills by strictly enforcing the measures from defined under the concessionaires of the LGU-Operated Waterworks System contrary to Section 164 and 165 of the Local Revenue Code.
observations.reason: Due to the current pandemic, the Municipal Treasury Office cannot conduct Barangay visits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish and maintain a GAD Database as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.reason: Collection of data for the Establishment of GAD Database is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System in order that the much-needed data and information essential for policy making can be verified and evaluated.
observations.reason: Evaluation Report was not submitted in the Office of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended that GAD Focal Point System immediately submit the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Auditor for evaluation and observe the timelines for submission of copies of GPB and AR to COA.

We recommended that Management:

Instruct the HRMO to require the JO/COS to submit Tax Identification Number (TIN) and Annual Registration Fee (ARF) upon hiring and renewal of contracts, respectively. 

Require the Municipal Accountant to compute, withhold and remit the respective percentage taxes of JO/COS Personnel for CY 2018, as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations. 

Capacitate the Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to attend seminars and trainings related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR Rulings.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to handle the required tasks.

observations.recommendation: 1.4	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement and effect immediately the adjustments in the accounts affected in order to present fairly the Cash-in-Bank-Local Currency Checking Account in the CY 2019 Statement of Financial Position.

2.8	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepare the above -mentioned certified list of taxpayers to be furnished the Municipal Accounting Office as basis for setting up the beginning balance of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax every year at an equal amount of one percent for each of the account out of the total amount due and collectible from taxpayers for a particular year.
2.9	We further recommended that subsidiary ledgers/taxpayers records be updated and maintained.

3.12	We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of its PPE, Inventory and Biological Assets at the end of every year in order that appropriate adjustments of the accounts affected be promptly effected, leading to a reliable and fair presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position.
We recommended that Management to submit within the reglementary period a complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.reason: The process of creating an Inventory Committee is still on-going. Thus, submission of complete and accurate RPCPPE within the reglementary period was not complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC to indicate in all purchase orders complete information, such as the mode of procurement, printed name of person signing the purchase order, date of receipt terms and condition, date of delivery, etc., as required in COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.reason: Lack of coordination between responsible parties hinders the preparation of the POs in the required form and substance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC to strictly adhere to the COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of approved contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days upon execution or issuance, respectively, to enable the timely review and inspection thereof.
observations.reason: Proximity of the official station of the Provincial Auditor’s Office hinders the delay of the  submission of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the assets as items of Property, Plant and Equipment and to recognize the subsequent depreciation thereof.
We recommended that the Management to strictly observe the provisions of Republic Act 9184 on the procurement of goods thru competitive bidding or other alternative modes of procurement.
observations.reason:
Lack of  knowledge and/or awareness of the BAC on the New Government Procurement Act or RA 9184, thus 
specific provisions of RA 9184 were not properly carried and observed in its procurement activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of Republic Act 9184 and not to refer to any brand names in the canvassing of prices to give equal opportunity to all brands which meets the characteristics, functionality and/or performance requirements set by the procuring agency.
observations.reason: Specific provisions of RA 9184 were not properly carried and  observed by the BAC.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the retention money as Performance/Bidders/Bail Bonds Payable instead of Accounts Payable.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Accountant to comply with the requirement of R.A. 10121 that the unexpended LDRRMF shall accrue to a special Trust Fund for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and Management activities within the next five (5) years, guided by the accounting and reporting procedures under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management implement exert extra effort to implement those projects programmed on their annual budget and finish it on target date for the timely delivery of services. Furthermore, the Management may fund other projects that are eligible under the 20% Development Fund using the budget originally intended for schools.
observations.reason: Some projects remained unimplemented during the year. Also, projects were not finished within the given timeframe.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC to strictly adhere to the COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of approved contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days upon execution or issuance, respectively, to enable the timely review and inspection thereof.
observations.managementAction: The BAC of the Municipality will submit the completeness of the documents as to copies of contract and purchase order within (5) five working days to COA Office.
observations.reason: The agency still did not submit contracts and POs to the Auditor within five (5) days upon execution and issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to submit within the reglamentary period a complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the BAC to indicate in all purchase orders complete information, such as the mode of procurement, printed name of person signing the purchase order, date of receipt terms and condition, date of delivery, etc., as required in COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that the BAC to strictly adhere to the COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of approved contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days upon execution or issuance, respectively, to enable the timely review and inspection thereof.
We recommended that the Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the assets as items of Property, Plant and Equipment and to recognize the subsequent depreciation thereof.
We recommended that the Management to strictly observe the provisions of Republic Act 9184 on the procurement of goods thru competitive bidding or other alternative modes of procurement.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of Republic Act 9184 and not to refer to any brand names in the canvassing of prices to give equal opportunity to all brands which meets the characteristics, functionality and/or performance requirements set by the procuring agency.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the retention money as Performance/Bidders/Bail Bonds Payable instead of Accounts Payable.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Accountant to comply with the requirement of R.A. 10121 that the unexpended LDRRMF shall accrue to a special Trust Fund for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and Management activities within the next five (5) years, guided by the accounting and reporting procedures under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the HRMO to require the JO/COS to submit Tax Identification Number (TIN) and Annual Registration Fee (ARF) upon hiring and renewal of contracts, respectively.
Require the Municipal Accountant to compute, withhold and remit the respective percentage taxes of JO/COS Personnel for CY 2018, as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations
Capacitate the Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to attend seminars and trainings related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR Rulings.

We recommended that Management implement exert extra effort to implement those projects programmed on their annual budget and finish it on target date for the timely delivery of services. Furthermore, the Management may fund other projects that are eligible under the 20% Development Fund using the budget originally intended for schools.

We recommended that Management strictly comply the following course of actions:

Conduct of complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be done by the Inventory Committee.

Maintain the required PPE ledger cards and making sure that all properties are completely reported in the inventory report.

Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to determine correctness and/or any losses occasioned by theft or other casualty. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the inventory report and accounting records, the necessary adjustment/corrections should be made.

Submit complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Coordination between Mun. Treasurer, Accountant, and Engineer to fast tract the inventory repost ASAP. Recommend that the Accounting office to established proper ledger cards.
observations.reason: Lack of cooperation among the employees. The Management was not able to form an inventory committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to effect the required reconciliation to present fairly the balance of Due to NGAS account.
observations.managementAction: the LGU conduct immediate action for the reconciliation of the Physical and Financial Accomplishment report of the Various project in our LGUs
observations.reason: The Management has already reconciled the physical and financial accomplishment report to the amounts reflected in the Financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the members of the Local School Board allocate and utilize the Special Education Fund for programs, projects and activities that adhere to the provisions stipulated in Section 272, Article 363 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: Through Local School Board meeting and given attention to Tubajon District Management that the SEF Utilization will strictly adhere for the welfare and benefits among the students and school programs/activities/project as hereby provided by the COA rules and regulation.
observations.reason: Lack of coordination

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly comply, and instruct the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the COA Auditor as required in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM of Tubajon together w/ the Local Accounting Office will promptly submit the Utilization report within (15) fifteen days after the end of each month and we adhere to the COA provision.
observations.reason: The Audit Team already receives monthly report on sources and utilization of DRRMF within 15 days after the end of each month.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the lacking Disbursement Voucher to the Audit Team, and, strictly comply with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the projects and activities charged in the unspent balance of LDRRMF in their annual LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: We submit also the separate PPA’s list of project and Activities which anchored to AIP and DRRM Plan that charge to unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to the record of the Local Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LGU to strictly adhere to the provision of Section 15, paragraph 3 of RA 9344.
observations.managementAction: The LGU strictly set aside the exact 1% share from IRA for LCPC of the Municipality

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Councils to submit Local Investment Plan for Children and LCPC Accomplishment Report to facilitate review and validation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will conduct immediate submission the LCPC and Accomplishment report to COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make timely remittance and adhere to the pertinent provisions of BIR Revenue Regulations No. 4-2002, and Section 80 of the Tax Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office will strictly implement the timely remittance for the withholding of taxes on procured of goods and services. So far the accounting office will remit the withheld taxes for compensation to the BIR it should be even. We adhere the BIR Laws.

observations.recommendation: Require the GAD Focal Point Person to submit the Gender and Development (GAD) Annual Plan and Budget as well as GAD Accomplishment Report to facilitate review and validation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU management will promptly submit the GAD Annual Plan and Budget with Accomplishment Report to COA Office and Facilitate the review and validation of the Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 37 of Republic Act 9003 in order to deliver the services and benefits to which the people of Tubajon are entitled to.
observations.managementAction: The clustered Sanitary landfill (SLF) from Libjo, Tubajon and Loreto will be constructed by the Provincial Government, since, 5th class Municipalities can’t afford for the construction of SLF. Currently the LGU has implementing the segregation at source and providing Material Recovery (MRF) to Brgy’s and Municipal MRF at the Mun. Eco Park
observations.reason: The LGU still do not have a sanitary landfill contrary to Section 37 of RA 9003 but provides MRF to brgys and has a Municipal MRF located at its Eco-Park.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to effect the required reconciliation to present fairly the balance of Due to NGAs account.

We recommended that the members of the Local School Board allocate and utilize the Special Education Fund for programs, projects and activities that adhere to the provisions stipulated in Section 272, Article 363 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017.

We recommend that Management strictly comply, and instruct the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the COA Auditor as required in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that Management submit the lacking Disbursement Voucher to the Audit Team, and, strictly comply with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the projects and activities charged in the unspent balance of LDRRMF in their annual LDRRMFIP.
Direct the LGU to strictly adhere to the provision of Section 15, paragraph 3 of RA 9344.
We recommend the Local Councils to submit Local Investment Plan for Children and LCPC Accomplishment Report to facilitate review and validation.

We recommend that the management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make timely remittance and adhere to the pertinent provisions of BIR Revenue Regulations No. 4-2002, and Section 80 of the Tax Revenue Code.
Require the GAD Focal Point Person to submit the Gender and Development (GAD) Annual Plan and Budget as well as GAD Accomplishment Report to facilitate review and validation.

We recommend that the BAC to strictly adhere to the COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of approved contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days upon execution or issuance, respectively, to enable the timely review and inspection thereof.
We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 37 of Republic Act 9003 in order to deliver the services and benefits to which the people of Tubajon are entitled to.

We recommended that Management strictly comply the following course of actions:

Conduct of complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be done by the Inventory Committee.

Maintain the required PPE ledger cards and making sure that all properties are completely reported in the inventory report.

Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to determine correctness and/or any losses occasioned by theft or other casualty. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the inventory report and accounting records, the necessary adjustment/corrections should be made.

Submit complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is looking for better and good governance in presenting the PPE aspect. We adhere the recommendation by the COA to make Inventory committee to focus the said problem, So far I instructed the Designated GSO Officer to communicate treasurer and accountant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and regularly compute, on a monthly basis, the depreciation of all depreciable assets, in order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Last Year the accounting office did not apply Depreciation due to further study of ECPAC system but this year 2017 we apply depreciation and any other adjustment made for depreciation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the financial assistance to LGUs for the construction of the infrastructure projects be covered with Memorandum of Agreement to provide bases for monitoring the proper use of such government funds and be liquidated by the implementing agencies through the submission of required documents mentioned in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Also, future financial assistance to barangays should be booked-up as Due from LGUs.
observations.managementAction: This year we adhere to follow the guidelines and we are giving assistance to Brgy.  w/ corresponding MOA and accomplishment from previous cash assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Procurement Officer-Designate to indicate in all purchase orders complete information required in COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: The PO actually is filled-up and signed by the AO concerned, if there is incomplete it’s a minimal manner. Furthermore, the office will receive the article which they had purchase.
observations.reason: The basic information in the POs are not completely filled-up, hence, the timeliness of the delivery based on the terms cannot be determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The LGU did not practice giving and paying PEI. Actually during post audit it was found out that the charging of the payroll is from General Fund, but the issuance of check is 20% Development Fund. So I instructed the Mun. Treasurer to return the error of disbursement to proper account.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of the concerned Accountable Officers with unliquidated balances without prejudice to the filing of appropriate sanctions as provided for under the above-mentioned provisions. Also, it is recommended that the Management should discontinue the practice of granting cash advance/s to any official or employees without first settling or liquidation thereof has been made of the previous cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting office Issued Demand letter

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management compel the Municipal Treasurer to immediately restitute the missing funds, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal charges, as the evidence may warrant. Moreover, we recommend also the immediate relief of accountable officer from duty to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Missing funds not yet restituted and accountable officer was not relieved from duty.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging to the 205 development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature   of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: We abide the joint memorandum circular No.2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DBM but there are some special projects that we need to maintain man power utilization like our reforestation and solid waste project, thus, requiring us to allocate for labor expenses.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2016.

observations.recommendation: 1.3	The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of the concerned Accountable Officers with unliquidated balances without prejudice to the filing of appropriate sanctions as provided for under the above-mentioned provisions. Also, it is recommended that the Management should discontinue the practice of granting cash advance/s to any official or employees without first settling or liquidation thereof has been made of the previous cash advance.

2.4	We recommend that the financial assistance to LGUs for the construction of the infrastructure projects be covered with Memorandum of Agreement to provide bases for monitoring the proper use of such government funds and be liquidated by the implementing agencies through the submission of required documents mentioned in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Also, future financial assistance to barangays should be booked-up as Due from LGUs.
3.3	We recommended that management direct the Procurement Officer-Designate to indicate in all purchase orders complete information required in COA Circular No. 96-010.

4.9	We recommend that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

5.5	We recommended the following course of actions:
Conduct of complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be done by the Inventory Committee.
Maintain the required PPE ledger cards and making sure that all properties are completely reported in the inventory report.
Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to determine correctness and/or any losses occasioned by theft or other casualty. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the inventory report and accounting records, the necessary adjustment/corrections should be made.
Submit complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.

6.7	We recommended that Management effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and regularly compute, on a monthly basis, the depreciation of all depreciable assets, in order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management establish specific internal control system and procedures on the purchase and issuance of Gasoline, Oil and Lubricants to government motor vehicles in compliance to COA Circular No. 77-61. Also, we recommended that Management should see to it that purchases of gasoline, oil, and lubricants should be duly supported by a  Fuel Consumption Report detailing the issuance of fuel per trip until the fuel purchased has been consumed with: (1) driver’s trip tickets that are completely filled up, and (2) serially numbered
observations.managementAction: So far the local government of Tubajon, in the Procurement of Gasoline, oil and lubricants for our Government Motors and vehicles, the Accounting Office always required the supporting documents for fuel consumption and trip tickets been done in any disbursements. But recently the internal control is done by the.
observations.reason: No specific office as internal control.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursement basis and see to it that the disbursements of government funds shall be subjected to withholding taxes in effecting payment for the procured goods and services pursuant to Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 of the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
.
observations.managementAction: Actually, the Local Government cannot totally stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursement but we implemented to withhold taxes to any reimbursement in purchases of goods and services.
observations.reason: Withholding of tax implemented.
